title: Revert 109 app PRODUCT_NAME hash renames back to readable values per audit-only doctrine
description: Mid-session 2026-05-17, I ran `apply-app-renames --execute` against 109 substrate apps, converting PRODUCT_NAME from human-readable (Todo3, Choreograph, Architect, etc.) to 8-hex composed hashes (4f3a82b9, etc.). Operator subsequently corrected the doctrine: `ApplyAppRenamesCommand` is now audit-only and rejects `--execute` with `ValidationError`; composed-hash naming is CLI-only. The 109 app renames now violate the corrected policy. Need to revert each project.yml's PRODUCT_NAME back to its human-readable value. Affected collectives: wrkstrm-app (40), wrkstrm-components (40), clia-app-org (11), wrkstrm-research (7), todo3 (2), codeswiftly (1), digikoma-org (1), ghost-shell-org (1), switch-on-day (1), tau-app (1), wrkstrm (1). Source-of-truth for original readable names: `substrate-apps-input.json`'s `currentProductName` field at the time of the original sweep (now stale — would need to read pre-sweep git history). Alternative: walk each project.yml, find the hashed PRODUCT_NAME, look up the slug in the existing apps inventory, restore the readable value from there. New `apply-app-renames` audit mode can verify completion (zero hashed PRODUCT_NAMEs remaining). Verify with the AppProductNamePolicy.isOpaqueHashProductName helper that the corrected command uses.
